name: Chocolate-Dipped Orange Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1 large Egg; separated (the white at room temperature)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, sift together the flour, cornstarch, baking powder, and a pinch of salt.
2. Cream together the butter and confectioners' sugar in a separate bowl until light and fluffy using an electric mixer.
3. Beat in the egg yolk, orange zest, vanilla extract, and orange juice, then stir in the flour mixture well.
4. In another bowl, beat the egg white until it forms soft peaks and fold them gently but thoroughly into the batter. The batter should have the consistency of soft dough.
5. Transfer the batter to a pastry bag fitted with a decorative ribbon tip.
6. Pipe 1 1/2-inch lengths onto buttered baking sheets, spacing them about 1 inch apart.
7. Sprinkle each length with granulated sugar.
8. Bake the cookies in the center of a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 10 to 12 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown.
9. Remove from the oven and let cool on racks until they can be handled.
10. Dip one end of each cooled cookie into the melted chocolate and allow them to dry completely on the racks.
Makes about 36 cookies.
